name: Shrimp Creole
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 tablespoon Fresh thyme or 2 teaspoons Dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp. Place shrimp heads (if available) and peels in a small saucepan with water. Bring to a slow boil over medium-high heat and let simmer for 15-20 minutes. Strain and discard the heads and peels.
2. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven or large heavy pot over medium-high heat. Add the onions, peppers, and celery. Sauté, stirring often, until vegetables are very soft, about 45 minutes.
3. Stir in the tomatoes, salt, ground red pepper, thyme (or dried thyme), sugar, and shrimp stock. Return to a simmer.
4. Reduce heat to medium and let simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally. The sauce can be prepared up to 2 days in advance and stored refrigerated; it improves after sitting a couple of days.
5. To serve, return the sauce to a simmer and add the shrimp. Cook until shrimp turn pink, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in green onions and parsley and cook for 1 more minute.
6. Serve over beds of rice on flat plates. Serves 6-8.
SHRIMP AND HAM OR TASSO JAMBALAYA:
Prepare Shrimp Creole as above, but add about a pound of cubed ham and a 4-ounce can of tomato sauce to the finished sauce and simmer an additional 45 minutes. Meanwhile, boil or steam 2-3 cups raw rice.
Finish the sauce (which will be your jambalaya base) by adding shrimp, green onions, and parsley as above. If using leftover Shrimp Creole, remove the shrimp and reheat the sauce with ham and tomato sauce for an additional 45 minutes before returning the shrimp to the pan at the end of cooking. Mix hot rice with the jambalaya base and serve. Sausage can replace the ham.
